creator: steinhelten@gmail.com
text:
We can take the pkg issue off the table. I failed to notice that the system time was not getting set, and RPIs do not have a battery clock.  After setting the time, pkg installed perfectly, so ignore that part, even though I didn’t immediately notice the cause/effect relationship.

OK, we can also chalk up a certain amount of my problems to my ignorance.  I’ll own  that.  I can see how I stepped on my own toes and confused myself in some instances.  There are, however, still peculiar things happening.

1) In the new default situation, /etc/aliases contains an example which is invalid for that configuration, though it is commented out, and specifically references sendmail:
# NOTE: /var/msgs and /var/msgs/bounds must be owned by sendmail's
#       DefaultUser (defaults to mailnull) for the msgs alias to work.
#
# msgs: "| /usr/bin/msgs -s"
2) The FreeBSD handbook, chapter 31.5.1, still talks about replacing sendmail with dma.  That should probably be rewritten to go the other way if DMA is going to be the default from now on.
3) After installing postfix-3.9.0 via pkg, aliases with spaces and parameters get processed correctly, albeit silently, despite the fact that NO CHANGE was made to /etc/mail/mailer.conf - whatever mechanism is being used to run something other than dma, it’s not using mailer.conf.
4) Having installed neither postfix nor sendmail on a fresh image, it looks like “real” sendmail is still on the system, because “/usr/libexec/sendmail/sendmail -bi” succeeds and creates /etc/mail/aliases.db despite the fact that dma won’t use it(nor postfix, should you happen to have installed it).  In fact, /usr/libexec/sendmail/sendmail is version 8.18.1, the same as what pkg would (re)install
# /usr/libexec/sendmail/sendmail -bt -d < /dev/null
Version 8.18.1
5) The error you get from dma when run as newaliases with an alias it doesn’t like first reports a syntax error on the wrong line number (off by one) and then  says, “could not parse aliases file `/etc/aliases': No such file or directory” which is not true - /etc/aliases is a sym link to “mail/aliases” which certainly is there and obviously, it could not have found a syntax error.
6) The mailer.conf manpage says that you should include
rmail           /usr/libexec/dma
but the default mailer.conf does not contain this.
7) The installation message from pkg for sendmail says mailer.conf should include the following
sendmail        /usr/local/sbin/sendmail
send-mail       /usr/local/sbin/sendmail
mailq           /usr/local/sbin/sendmail
newaliases      /usr/local/sbin/sendmail
hoststat        /usr/local/sbin/sendmail
purgestat       /usr/local/sbin/sendmail
but the man page doesn't mention send-mail, hoststat or purgestat

So, from this, I wonder if I can revert from DMA to “real” sendmail just by changing the contents of /etc/mail/mailer.conf, though I suspect that "real" sendmail may be omitted from some future release and would have to be installed by pkg or ports.  Also, you don't get the message about updating mailer.conf unless you install the sendmail pkg.  Oh, and the pkg installs cyrus-sasl and cyrus-sasl-saslauthd and creates the user and group smmsp - so it's really not clear to me if I need to install the package in order to revert to "real" sendmail or not.
